missions.title: The Voice of the Agents: Capability Vote and Next Priority Mission
missions.description: Run a structured capability vote across all active roles. The software developer must collect one-by-one input from each role, document what they need most, and create a follow-up mission for the highest-priority improvement.

metadata.goal: Use cross-role feedback to decide the most urgent next capability for the autonomous system
initial_hypotheses: Introduce a dedicated QA role and workflow.
Introduce a Kanban board to track planned, in-progress, and done tasks.
Adopt stronger Git-based project/ticket workflow and make push readiness a priority.
workflow_constraints: The software_developer is the owner of this mission output.
The software_developer must call roles one by one and gather notes from each role individually.
No combined summary is valid unless each role has a dedicated contribution section.
All role feedback must be traceable in the final document.

deliverables.description: Role-by-role notes, capability proposals, rationale, urgency ranking, and recommended next action.
Add a new follow-up mission that implements the highest-urgency capability identified in docs/voice-of-the-agents.md.

documentation_requirements: Include consulted roles and timestamped notes per role.
Include a voting table with capability, supporting roles, urgency, impact, and confidence.
Include a final recommendation section with the top priority and why it won.
Include a proposed mission_id, title, description, and acceptance criteria for the follow-up mission.
acceptance_criteria: Every active role available in the runtime has a dedicated documented contribution.
docs/voice-of-the-agents.md exists and contains role notes plus a prioritized decision.
A new mission is added to config/missions.json to implement the selected top priority.
The follow-up mission is concrete enough to be executed without re-interviewing roles.
